skills.title: Token Architecture — The Flawless 3-Tier Derivation Structure
skills.summary: The canonical, opinionated PRIMITIVE -> SEMANTIC -> COMPONENT variable structure (one collection per tier, theme as Semantic modes, strictly one-tier-down aliases, tightly scoped bound tokens) that a code-gen pipeline consumes deterministically — plus the Plugin-API-detectable structural laws that seed the linter.
skills.when_to_use: Read before any write_variables call that creates or edits color/dimension tokens or collections, before wiring theming/modes, and whenever lint_tokens flags a token-structure error. It defines WHAT structure to build and in what order (Primitives -> Semantic -> Component, lint between each).

skills.title: Variable Scopes — Binding the Right Token to the Right Property
skills.summary: Map Figma's 22 VariableScopes to correct usage so every non-primitive token binds only to its real property — the guardrail that makes design→code output correct by construction.
skills.when_to_use: Read this whenever you create or edit any non-primitive variable (semantic or component token) with write_variables, whenever you set or change a variable's `scopes`, and before you bind a variable to a node property. Also read it before running lint_tokens / lint_design_system so you know what "correctly scoped" means. If a token's scope is left at the default ALL_SCOPES, this skill applies.

skills.title: Theming with Modes — Light/Dark and Multi-Brand
skills.summary: Model light/dark and multi-brand theming as modes on the semantic layer with a primitive→semantic→component alias chain, so one mode switch flips the whole system and design→code output stays deterministic.
skills.when_to_use: Read BEFORE creating or restructuring any themed design system in Figma — light/dark, multi-brand, or any variable set that must change appearance by mode. Also read when a lint_tokens / lint_design_system run flags a mode-count, alias-chain, raw-literal-in-mode, or axis-mixing violation, so you can self-correct instead of guessing.

skills.title: Component & Variant Structure That Survives Code-Gen
skills.summary: Build Figma component/variant sets whose property structure maps 1:1 to code component props, so design→code export stops mangling components.
skills.when_to_use: Read before creating or editing any component or variant set, before adding any component property (variant / boolean / instance-swap / slot), before wiring slots or instance overrides, and before exporting a component to code. Also read whenever `lint_design_system` flags component or variant structure.

skills.title: Design→Code Correctness: Token Structure That Survives Code-Gen
skills.summary: Build a 3-tier token structure (Primitives→Semantic→Component) where every visual property is bound to a scoped, codeSyntax-carrying semantic token, so Figma Dev Mode / code-gen emits clean, stable, themeable code the first time — and self-correct via the linter before handoff.
skills.when_to_use: Read BEFORE authoring any variables/tokens or components that will be handed to code, and again as a pre-handoff gate. Trigger it whenever the goal is "design→code," the generated code emits raw hex / raw values, a design lacks a semantic layer, codeSyntax is missing, or Dev Mode surfaces the wrong or unscoped tokens. Pair with the structure linter (lint_tokens / lint_design_system).

skills.title: Accessibility & Naming Conventions (niche DS quality)
skills.summary: Enforce WCAG/APCA contrast on semantic fg/bg token pairs, minimum text sizes, a strict kebab/slash naming grammar, and orphan/duplicate/unused-token hygiene — all locally detectable via Figma Plugin API 1.130 so build→lint→fix replaces one-component-at-a-time trial-and-error.
skills.when_to_use: Read this before or right after any write_variables run that creates/renames color, text-size, or semantic tokens; before a design→code handoff; when building semantic color pairs or text styles; and whenever lint_design_system / lint_tokens / lint_a11y reports a naming, contrast, tiering, or orphan finding. It is the paired knowledge doc for the accessibility + naming linter checks.
